# UC-22.7.26 · Encryption in Transit — Deprecated TLS on Internal APIs

## Description

Parses load balancer or proxy access logs for deprecated TLS handshakes supporting data security requirements.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes proxy and sourcetypes nginx:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extraction; (2) emergency patch plan for legacy clients; (3) document compensating network segmentation. Indexes required: `index=proxy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=nginx:access`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: nginx, F5, or AWS ALB access logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `nginx:access` ssl_protocol.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extraction; (2) emergency patch plan for legacy clients; (3) document compensating network segmentation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype=nginx:access earliest=-24h
| where ssl_protocol IN ("TLSv1","TLSv1.1","SSLv3")
| stats count by src_ip, ssl_protocol, uri
```

Understanding this SPL

**Encryption in Transit — Deprecated TLS on Internal APIs (PR.DS-01)** — Parses load balancer or proxy access logs for deprecated TLS handshakes supporting data security requirements.

Documented **Data sources**: `nginx:access` ssl_protocol. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): nginx, F5, or AWS ALB access logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: nginx:access. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype=nginx:access, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where ssl_protocol IN ("TLSv1","TLSv1.1","SSLv3")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, ssl_protocol, uri** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar chart

## SPL

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype=nginx:access earliest=-24h
| where ssl_protocol IN ("TLSv1","TLSv1.1","SSLv3")
| stats count by src_ip, ssl_protocol, uri
```

## Visualization

Table, Bar chart. Key fields: `src_ip`, `ssl_protocol`, `uri`.
